title: Prompt Injection, Tool Output Trust, Exfiltration, and Safe Rendering · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved agent trajectory contains one plausible model, schema, tool, authority, state, loop, memory, concurrency, injection, evaluation, telemetry, deployment, or recovery defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in instruction-data separation and agent security gateway and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Represent application policy, user instructions, retrieved data, tool output, memory, and provider metadata with explicit authority and non-executable boundaries.
Test direct and indirect goal hijacking, authority spoofing, hidden text, encoded payloads, multimodal instructions, and tool-result injection.
Treat tool names, content, links, files, citations, metadata, error text, and nested model output as untrusted typed data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug agent engineering team handles case 4409 during a controlled support-operations rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate instructions from data, resist direct and indirect injection, constrain tools, prevent exfiltration, and encode rendered output; model output and tool arguments remain untrusted until validated; then change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Safe Output Rendering
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-safe-output-rendering-usurkq" that uses case 9878 to separate instructions from data, resist direct and indirect injection, constrain tools, prevent exfiltration, and encode rendered output. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Safe Output Rendering.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Safe Output Rendering into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Safe Output Rendering, keep Safe Output Rendering connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-safe-output-rendering-usurkq
# Competency: agentpy-safe-output-rendering.
# Case 9878: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure.
# Changed case: inject cancellation, retry, repeated-call, checkpoint, or replay failure and recover without duplicate effects.
def evidence_agentpy_safe_output_rendering_usurkq(untrusted_inputs: int = 40, rejected_instructions: int = 18, admitted_data: int = 22, exfiltration_events: int = 0, unsafe_renderings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_usurkq = "usurkq-qkrusu-9878"
    assert evidence_variant_usurkq
    evidence_axes_usurkq = (
        "authorityusurkq",
        "interaction9878",
        "toolqkrusu",
        "stateusurkq9878",
        "evaluation9878usurkq",
        "recoveryqkrusu9878",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_usurkq)) == 6
    if untrusted_inputs < 1 or rejected_instructions < 0 or admitted_data < 0:
        return False, untrusted_inputs, "security-input-count"
    if rejected_instructions + admitted_data != untrusted_inputs:
        return False, rejected_instructions, "instruction-data-reconciliation"
    if exfiltration_events > 0:
        return False, exfiltration_events, "data-exfiltration"
    if unsafe_renderings > 0:
        return False, unsafe_renderings, "unsafe-output-rendering"
    assert rejected_instructions + admitted_data == untrusted_inputs and exfiltration_events == 0
    return True, rejected_instructions, "secured-agent-boundary"
